name: Caramelized Pear Charlottes W/Persimmon
recipeIngredient: 2 Firm-ripe Bosc pears, peel, core, cut into 6ths
1 tbsp Sugar plus additional to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small non-stick skillet melt 1 tablespoon butter over moderately high heat until foam begins to subside.
2. Saute pears, turning occasionally, until golden, about 10 minutes.
3. Sprinkle 1 tbsp sugar over pears and cook, stirring once or twice, until golden brown and caramelized, about 1 minute.
4. Puree pears in a food processor or blender until smooth and transfer to a bowl. Pear puree may be made up to 1 day ahead and chilled, covered.
5. Preheat oven to 400°F.
6. In a small saucepan melt remaining 1/2 stick butter.
7. Cut 2 bread slices into rounds the same size as bottoms of two half-c charlotte molds, ramekins, or custard cups and cut 2 bread slices into rounds the same size as tops of molds, reserving bread trimmings.
8. In a small food processor grind trimmings fine and stir into pear puree.
9. Brush smaller rounds on both sides with some melted butter and place them in bottom of molds.
10. Cut remaining bread slices into 1-inch squares and discard crusts. Brush squares on both sides with some remaining butter and place in sides of molds, overlapping slightly and pressing gently against the side of the mold.
11. Divide enough pear puree between lined molds to fill them to within 1/8 inch of the top.
12. Brush remaining 2 bread rounds on both sides with remaining butter and place tops of molds, pressing down gently but firmly to cover filling completely.
13. Charlottes may be prepared up to this point up to 3 hours ahead and chilled, covered.
14. Bake charlottes for 20 minutes, or until bread is golden brown.
15. While charlottes are baking, peel and core 2 persimmons and puree with lemon juice and additional sugar in a food processor.
16. Divide persimmon puree between 2 dessert plates and carefully invert charlottes onto it.
17. Cut remaining persimmon into wedges and arrange around charlottes.
